tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #57773) by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed The `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` library (v1.0.0-beta.1) was introduced as a new provisioning package for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, generated using the new provisioning generator. It includes resource types such as `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, and `AdvancedThreatProtection`. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is completely empty** — no code snippets demonstrating how to create a MySQL Flexible Server or related resources - **No sample test file exists** — `tests/Samples/` directory has no `.cs` files; there are no snippet-backed code examples - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication works with provisioning libraries - **"Key concepts" section contains only one generic sentence** — no description of the specific resource types available or how they relate to each other - **"Next steps" section is empty** — no links to related resources or documentation
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` (edit) Replace the empty `## Examples` section with a code snippet placeholder, and fill in the empty sections. Follow the pattern from `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/README.md` as a reference. Replace the current README content with: ```markdown # Azure Provisioning MySql client library for .NET Azure.Provisioning.MySql simplifies declarative resource provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers in .NET. ## Getting started ### Install the package Install the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/): ```dotnetcli dotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.MySql --prerelease ``` ### Prerequisites > You must have an [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/). ### Authenticate the Client This library provisions Azure infrastructure using the Azure Developer CLI (azd) or Azure Bicep. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling — no explicit client authentication code is required in the provisioning definition itself. ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. The main resource types provided by this library are: - **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — Represents an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance - **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — Represents a database within a flexible server - **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — Manages firewall rules to control access to the server - **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — Manages server configuration parameters - **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — Configures Azure Active Directory administrators for the server - **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** / **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`** — Manages server backups - **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Manages scheduled maintenance windows - **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Configures Advanced Threat Protection for the server - **`MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`** — Manages private endpoint connections ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server This example demonstrates how to create a MySQL Flexible Server with administrator login credentials. ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic ``` ## Troubleshooting - File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues). - Check [previous questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure+.net) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags. ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/mysql/flexible-server/overview) - Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the base provisioning library - See the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/) for deployment tooling ## Contributing For details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing guide][cg]. This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit . When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the bot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories using our CLA. This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact with any other questions or comments. [cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md [coc]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ [coc_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/ ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with a test demonstrating how to create a basic MySQL Flexible Server. Use `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/tests/Samples/` as a reference if it exists, otherwise model after `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`. The test file should: - Be in namespace `Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests` - Use `[Test]` attribute (NUnit) - Wrap the snippet content with `#region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` / `#endregion` - Demonstrate creating an `Infrastructure`, adding parameters (admin login, admin password), creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` with `MySqlFlexibleServerSku`, `MySqlFlexibleServerVersion`, `MySqlFlexibleServerStorage`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties`, and `MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability`, then adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` Example structure for `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning.Primitives; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests; public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [Category("Samples")] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); ProvisioningParameter adminLogin = new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator username of the server." }; infra.Add(adminLogin); ProvisioningParameter adminPass = new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator password of the server.", IsSecure = true }; infra.Add(adminPass); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new(nameof(server)) { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default, Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, AdministratorLogin = adminLogin, AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new(nameof(database)) { Parent = server, Name = "mydb" }; infra.Add(database); #endregion } } ``` **README snippet placeholder location:** In `README.md` under `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`, after the description line, insert: ``` ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic ``` ``` (a closing ` ``` ` follows — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the content) **Snippet name:** `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` is currently missing or has empty content for the following sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty — add authentication guidance | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — add resource type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ Empty — add code snippet | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty — add links to related resources | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from the new test file and injects the code into `README.md` placeholder blocks. Check `git diff` after running to confirm README was updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — only needed if any public types or members were added or changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [MySql] Docs: Add examples and key concepts to Azure.Provisioning.MySql README
